hadiths.ar:
2184  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم حَّان ِ وُّج ُّوةَّ إَّلن  َّاسِ  
ق َّا ِذ َّإ ق َّق َّذَّإ رَّج ُّلاً ق َّذْ عَّو َّذَّ ن َّق ْسَّهُّ إَّلْحَّح  َّ ق َّالَّ إ َّجَّذُّهُّمَّا لِصَّاجِن ِهِ ي َّا ق ُّلاَّن ُّ مَّا  ق َّعَّلَّ ق ُّلاَّن   ق َّالَّ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّعْلَّمُّ ق َّالَّ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ 
إ َّجَّذُّهُّمَّا إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ جَّن َّسَّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّح ِّ ق َّق ْز  ق َّا َّع ْن ِهِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ جَّن َّسَّهُّ ذَّي ْن   ق َّاق ْص ِ عَّن ْهُّ ذَّي ْ ن َّهُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ جَّن َّسَّهُّ مَّزَّض    
ق َّاس ْق ِهِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ جَّن َّسَّ هُّ مَّوْب   ق َّاع ْق ِزْ لَّهُّ وَّ إِرْجَّمْه
hadiths.en:
Abu Ja’far {a.s} would never turn away a beggar on the Day of Arafah.   
[AL SADUQ] 
Whoever frees a servant on the eve of the Day of Arafah, it suffices as the Hajj of Islam 
for the servant, and the master receives two rewards: the reward of freeing the servant 
and the reward of Hajj.  
It has been narrated regarding a servant freed on the Day of Arafah that if they reach 
either of the two sacred stations (Arafah or Muzdalifah), they have attained Hajj.   
The most sinful among the people of Arafah is the one who departs from Arafah while 
believing they have not been forgiven, meaning one who despairs of the mercy of Allah 
{SWT}, Mighty and Majestic.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 142 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 223 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 403 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 556 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol. 93, p. 179 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 254 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2183
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٨٣

hadiths.ar:
2185  - وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  إ َّلْف ِ ص ِعْف ِ مِي ْلِهِ وَّ 
إ ِذ َّإ ذَّعَّا لِن َّق ْسِهِ كَّان َّث ْ لَّهُّ وَّإجِذَّة   ق َّمِان َّه ُّ إ َّلْف ٍ مَّص ْمُّون َّه   ج َّي ْز  مِن ْ وَّإجِذَّة ٍ لأَّ ي ُّذْرَّى ي ُّسْي َّح َّاب ُّ لَّهُّ إ َّمْ لأ
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"On the eve of Arafah, Allah {SWT}, Mighty and Majestic, sends two angels who examine 
the faces of the people. If they find a man who has accustomed himself to performing 
Hajj but is absent, one of them says to the other: 'O so -and-so, what happened to so-
and-so?'  
The other responds: 'Allah {SWT} knows best.'  
Then one of them says: 'O Allah {SWT}, if poverty has kept him from Hajj, enrich him. If 
debt has kept him, relieve him of his debt. If illness has kept him, cure him. And if death 
has kept him, forgive him and have mercy on him.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 238 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2184
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٨٤

hadiths.ar: 2186  - وَّ مَّن ْ ذَّعَّا لِأ َّرْن َّعِي ن َّ رَّج ُّلاً مِن ْ إ ِج ْوَّإن ِهِ ق َّن ْلَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّذْعُّوَّ لِن َّق ْسِهِ إُّسْي ُّح ِي ث َّ لَّهُّ ق ِي هِمْ وَّ ق ِى  ن َّق ْسِهِ   »
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "When a person prays for their brother in their absence, a 
caller from the Throne announces, 'For you is one hundred thousand times the like of 
it.' But when they pray for themselves, they receive only one.  
Therefore, one hundred thousand guaranteed is better than one uncertain, as it is not 
known whether their personal supplication will be answered or not."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2185
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٨٥

hadiths.en:
Whoever prays for forty of their brothers before praying for themselves will 
have their supplication answered both for them and for themselves.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2186
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٨٦

hadiths.ar:
2188   -  وَّ إ ِن  َّ إ َّن ْوَّإب َّ إَّلس َّمَّاءِ، لأَّ ي ُّع ْلَّق ُّ ي ِلْك َّ إَّلل َّي ْلَّه َّ لِأ َّصْوَّإب ِ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ لَّهُّمْ ذَّوِى    كَّذَّوِى ِّ إَّلن  َّحْ لِ ي َّق ُّولُّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ  
«كُّمْ 
ذ ُّن ُّون ُّهُّ وَّ ي َّع ْق ِزُّ لِمَّن ْ إ َّرَّإذَّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّع ْق َّزَّ لَّه    
ق ا ذ إ إر ذجم إلن اس ق لم ي ق ذروإ على إ ن  ي ي ق ذموإ ولأ ي ي ا ج زوإ كن زوإ ق ا ن  إلي كن ي ز ي ذ هث  ن الص ع اط.
hadiths.en:
Whoever passes between the narrow paths of Mina without arrogance, Allah 
{SWT} will forgive their sins.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2187
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٨٧

hadiths.ar:
2189  - وَّ إَّلْحَّاح  ُّ إ ِذ َّإ وَّق َّف َّ ن ِالْمَّس ْعَّزِ ج َّزَّح َّ مِن ْ ذ ُّن ُّون ِهِ    
وإلوق وف  ن عزق ه  سن ه ، ون المس عز ق زي ص ه . وما من  عمل إ ق ص ل ي وم إلن حز من  ذم مسق وك ، إ و مس ى  ق ى  ن ز إلوإلذي ن  إ و ذ ى   - 
رجم ق اطغ ي ا ج ذ  علي ه ن الق ص ل وي ن ذإ ة ن السلام، إ و رج ل إ طعم من  صالح ن سكه ي م ذعا إ لى ن ق ي ي ه ج ي زإن ه من  إلي ي امى وإ هل 
إلمسكن ه  وإلمملوك  وي عاهذ إلأسزإء.
hadiths.en:
Indeed, on that night (the eve of Arafah), the gates of the heavens are not 
closed to the voices of the believers, whose supplications resound like the buzzing of 
bees. Allah {SWT}, Mighty and Majestic, says: 'I am your Lord {AZJ}, and you are My 
servants. You have fulfilled My right, and it is incumbent upon Me to answer you.'  
On that night, He {SWT} removes the sins of those whom He {SWT} wills and then forgives 
those whom He {SWT} wills to forgive.  
[AL SADUQ] 
When the crowds become too dense, and people cannot move forward or backward, 
they should recite Takbir (Allahu Akbar), for Takbir relieves congestion.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2188
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٨٨

hadiths.ar: 2190  - وَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ زَّإطِ
hadiths.en:
"The pilgrim, when they stand at Al-Mash'ar (Muzdalifah), is cleansed of their 
sins."  
[AL SADUQ] 
Standing at Arafah is a Sunnah, while standing at Al -Mash'ar is an obligation. On the 
Day of Sacrifice (Eid al -Adha), there is no deed better than spilling the blood of a 
sacrificial animal, walking to serve one's parents, reconciling with estranged relatives 
by treating them with kindness and initiating greetings, or a p erson who shares the 
meat of their sacrifice with their neighbors, including orphans, the needy, servants, 
and prisoners, ensuring their well-being.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2189
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٨٩

hadiths.ar:
2191  -  وَّ ج َّاءَّب ْ إ ُّم ُّ سَّلَّمَّه َّ رَّص ِى َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّن ْهَّا إ ِلَّى إَّلن  َّن ِى ِّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق َّق َّالَّث ْ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إ لل َّهِ ي َّحْص ُّزُّ إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى 
وَّ لَّي ْسَّ عِن ْذِى  ي َّمَّن ُّ إَّلْأ ُّص ْحِي  َّه ِ ق َّا َّسْي َّق ْزِض ُّ وَّ إ ُّص َّحِّى  ق َّق َّال ى  ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ذَّي ْن   مَّق ْص ِى
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, 
said: "Choose the best of your sacrifices, for they are your mounts on the Sirat 
(Bridge)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 438 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 1119 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 209 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 7, p. 276 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 296 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2190
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٠

hadiths.en:
Umm Salamah, may Allah {SWT} be pleased with her, came to the Prophet, 
peace and blessings be upon him and his family, and said:  
"O Messenger of Allah {SWT}, the Day of Sacrifice is approaching, and I do not have the 
means to afford a sacrifice. Should I borrow and offer a sacrifice?"  
He {saws} replied: "Borrow and offer a sacrifice, for it is a debt that will be repaid."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 210 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2191
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩١

hadiths.ar:
2193  - وَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  مِن ْ ذَّمِهَّا ي َّع ْق ِزُّ إَّلل َّهُّ لَّهُّ 
عَّلَّى ذ َّلِك
hadiths.en:
And the owner of the sacrifice is forgiven at the very first drop of its blood that 
falls.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2192
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٢

hadiths.en:
Abu Ja’far {a.s} said: "The practice of marking the sacrificial animal (Ish’ar) was 
deemed good because at the very first drop of its blood that falls, Allah {SWT} forgives 
the person for that act."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 434 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 206 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 558 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 278 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 101 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2193
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٣

hadiths.en:
"Whoever restrains their eyes, tongue, and hands during the Days of 
Tashreeq, Allah {SWT}, Mighty and Majestic, will record for them the reward of a Hajj in 
the following year."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2194
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٤

hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, 
said: "Casting the pebbles at the Jamarat is a provision for the Day of Judgment."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 1081 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 
55 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 263 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2195
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٥

hadiths.ar:
2197  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم إ رَّمَّاهَّا إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ُّ 
إِلْي َّق َّق َّهَّا إَّلْمَّلَّك ُّ وَّ إ ِذ َّإ رَّمَّاهَّا إَّلْكَّاق ِزُّ ق َّالَّ إَّلس  َّي ْطَّان ُّ ن ِاسْي ِك َّ مَّا رَّمَّي ْث
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, 
said: "When the pilgrim casts the pebbles at the Jamarat, they are cleansed of their 
sins."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 1081 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2196
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٦

hadiths.ar:
2198   -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ق ِي َّامَّه ِ وَّ كُّل ُّ س َّعْزَّة ٍ لَّهَّا 
لِسَّان   طَّلْق   ي ُّلَّن ِّى  ن ِاسْمِ صَّاجِن ِهَّا
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever casts the pebbles at the Jamarat will have a major destructive sin removed 
for each pebble. When a believer casts it, the angel catches it. But when a disbeliever 
casts it, Satan says mockingly, 'You did not truly strike me.'"   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 1081 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2197
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٧

hadiths.ar: 2199  - وَّ إِسْي َّع ْق َّزَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ لِلْمُّحَّلِّق ِي ن َّ ي َّلاَّب َّ مَّز َّإب ٍ وَّ لِلْمُّق َّصِّزِي ن َّ مَّز ة ً.
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "When a believer shaves their 
head at Mina and buries the hair, they will come on the Day of Judgment, and every 
hair will have a fluent tongue calling out the Talbiyah in the name of its owner."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 502 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 14, p. 1199 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 220 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2198
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٨

hadiths.ar:
2200  - وَّ رُّوِى  
ولأ ي ح ور  للصزورة  إ ن  ي ق صز، وعلي ه إلحلق .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, 
sought forgiveness for those who shaved their heads three times and for those who 
trimmed their hair once.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 224 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2199
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢١٩٩

hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: "Whoever shaves their head at Mina will have a light for 
every hair on the Day of Judgment."  
[AL SADUQ] 
It is not permissible for a first -time pilgrim ( Sarooorah) to trim their hair; they are 
obligated to shave.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 14, p. 1208 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 14, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2200
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢٢٠٠

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the words of Allah 
{SWT}, Mighty and Majestic: "Then whoever hastens to leave in two days, there is no sin 
upon him; and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him " (Surah Al-Baqarah, 2:203).  
Imam {a.s} said: "They return forgiven, with no sin upon them."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tafsir al-Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 99 • Tafsir al-Burhan, Vol. 1, 
p. 439 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 315 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 10, p. 160 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2201
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢٢٠١

hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: "They emerge from their sins as pure as the day their 
mother gave birth to them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 63, H. 2202
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON VIRTUES OF HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٢٢٠٢
